violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Observed food employees putting on gloves without washing their hands. Also, observed food employees begin food preparation after being outside of the kitchen without washing their hands first. Hands should be washed before beginning food preparation and before putting on gloves.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. Wiping cloths found stored in buckets that did not register sanitizer when tested. Wiping cloths should be stored in a sanitizing solution between uses or laundered.--In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Scoop found in ice bin with the handle in contact with the ice. Scoops should be stored so handles do not come in contact with the item being served.

Hot storage of potentially hazardous food. — --Hot food storage facilities/equipment was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining temperature of potentially hazardous food at 140F or greater. Hot hold well with rice and refried beans was found not working properly. After being heated to 165-200F before being placed in the well, items were found 2 hours later at 110-125F, set on high. **** Recommended marking the items in this well with the time that they are placed in the well and then discard them after 4 hours until well is fixed or replaced.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. New dry storage area has wooden shelving. Wood is an absorbent material that needs to be sealed with paint or other sealant.

Handling of clean equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not handled in a manner to prevent contamination of food-contact surface. Observed food employee carrying a stack of inverted, clean drinking glasses with his hand inside the bottom glass. Clean glasses should not be handled by the food contact surface.

Handwashing lavatory supplies. — --Handwash lavatory was not supplied with supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder or bar soap. Hand soap was not available at the hand washing sink. Soap should be available at all times.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. Cooked chicken and refried beans were found in the reach-in cooler at 46F. Potentially hazardous food items should be maintained at 41F or below. ***Chicken and refried beans should be discarded.

Temperature measuring devices. — --Temperature measuring device was not provided. Thermometer was not available in the refrigerator with salsas. Thermometers should be available for monitoring temperatures of all refrigerated devices.
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Observed employee remove a pan from the dishwasher and rinse it before hanging it on the wall. Equipment should not be rinsed after it is sanitized.

Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. Strips for testing the chlorine sanitizing solution in the dishwasher were not available during inspection. Strips should be available to assure proper solution strength is used to sanitize equipment.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. Dishwasher sanitizing solution was found at 0ppm during inspection. Chlorine solutions should be at least 50ppm for sanitizing food contact surfaces.
